title: Verb てからというもの
titleEn: Expresses that ever since a certain event happened, a condition has continued until now.
structure: Verb-て form + からというもの
structureEn: Verb-て form + からというもの
explanation: Expresses that ever since a certain event happened, a condition has continued until now. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The てからというもの grammar point indicates that from the time a certain action happened, a significant change or condition has persisted continuously. It emphasizes that the new situation contrasts with what came before.

title: Verb てこそ
titleEn: Emphasizes 'only by doing something, a certain result or realization can occur.'
explanation: Emphasizes 'only by doing something, a certain result or realization can occur.' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てこそ grammar point is used to highlight that only by performing the verb in て-form can a certain important outcome be achieved or recognized. It often translates as 'only by (doing)' or 'only when (doing).'

title: Verb ては
titleEn: Expresses a repeated pattern or cycle, often implying a frustrating outcome; 'whenever X happens, Y happens again.'
explanation: Expresses a repeated pattern or cycle, often implying a frustrating outcome; 'whenever X happens, Y happens again.' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar ～ては is used to show that whenever one action or situation occurs, another action or situation repeatedly follows. It can also be used in an alternating pattern: 'do this, then that, then this, then that.'

title: Verb ては Verb
titleEn: Expresses a repeated pattern of action; translates as 'every time', 'whenever', etc.
explanation: Expresses a repeated pattern of action; translates as 'every time', 'whenever', etc. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar point is used when you want to express a repeated or cyclical pattern of actions or events. It can be translated as 'every time', 'whenever', or 'if at all'. The typical formation is a verb in the て-form, followed by は, and then another verb (often in the same tense or a continuing action).

title: Verb てまでも
titleEn: Expresses 'even if it means doing...', 'no matter what it takes'.
explanation: Expresses 'even if it means doing...', 'no matter what it takes'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar point is used when the speaker wants to emphasize the lengths they would go to in order to achieve something. It attaches to the verb's て-form to mean 'even if it means doing X', 'even at the cost of X', or 'no matter what it takes'.

title: Verb てみせる
explanation: 'Show by doing', 'demonstrate', 'prove by action'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～てみせる grammar point is used to express the action of demonstrating or proving something by actually doing it. It can be translated as 'show by doing', 'demonstrate', or 'prove by action' in English.

title: Verb てやまない
titleEn: Used with certain emotional/mental verbs to show a strong, unceasing emotion: 'can't stop', 'never cease to'.
structure: Verb-て form + やまない (common with certain verbs)
structureEn: Verb-て form + やまない (common with certain verbs)
explanation: Used with certain emotional/mental verbs to show a strong, unceasing emotion: 'can't stop', 'never cease to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar ～てやまない is typically used with verbs like 願う, 祈る, 愛する, 尊敬する, 期待する, etc., expressing a deep, continuous emotional state (love, respect, hope, prayer). It emphasizes that the feeling 'never ceases.'

title: Verb ないではおかない
titleEn: Means 'cannot help but do', 'will definitely do', or 'is bound to happen'.
structure: Verb-ない form + ではおかない
structureEn: Verb-ない form + ではおかない
explanation: Means 'cannot help but do', 'will definitely do', or 'is bound to happen'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The pattern Verb ないではおかない indicates that something definitely occurs as a logical or emotional consequence. It can be translated as 'cannot not do', 'can’t help but do', or 'will inevitably do'. The verb is put in the negative form followed by ないではおかない.

title: Verb ないではすまない
titleEn: 'Cannot end without doing', 'must do'—an unavoidable moral or social necessity.
structure: Verb-ない form + ではすまない
structureEn: Verb-ない form + ではすまない
explanation: 'Cannot end without doing', 'must do'—an unavoidable moral or social necessity. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar Verb ないではすまない expresses that something must be done (often for moral, social, or personal reasons) and cannot be left undone. It implies an obligation or inevitability, akin to 'I can't get away without doing it.'

title: Verb ないまでも
titleEn: 'Even if not to the extent of...', 'although not completely...'.
explanation: 'Even if not to the extent of...', 'although not completely...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The pattern ないまでも is used when acknowledging that something doesn’t reach a certain extreme, but at least some part or degree of it applies. Often translated as 'even if not entirely...' or 'though not as far as...'.
